title: IBS symptoms
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Requires clinical assessment and examination for suspected-cancer features and inflammatory markers for inflammatory bowel disease, with referral when present; specific cancer-feature fields require their own exact source.
examination.rationale: Was reviewed for the requirement to assess cancer and inflammatory-bowel-disease features; no specific legacy alarm item is mapped without its own exact source.
examination.evidence_extract: Requires clinical assessment and examination for suspected-cancer features and inflammatory markers for inflammatory bowel disease, with referral when present; specific cancer-feature fields require their own exact source.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management

investigations.text: Supports at least 6 months of abdominal pain or discomfort, bloating, bowel-habit change, relation to defaecation or stool frequency or form, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus, and selected extra-intestinal symptoms.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped adult IBS symptom terms, abdominal pain, bloating, bowel-pattern, stool-pattern, food or stress context, urgency, straining, presets, and structured history with diagnostic qualifiers preserved.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports at least 6 months of abdominal pain or discomfort, bloating, bowel-habit change, relation to defaecation or stool frequency or form, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus, and selected extra-intestinal symptoms.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management

investigations.text: Supports full blood count, ESR or plasma viscosity, CRP, and coeliac antibodies in people meeting IBS criteria and identifies tests not required solely to confirm IBS.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed blood, CBC, CRP, and coeliac-serology fields in people meeting IBS criteria.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports full blood count, ESR or plasma viscosity, CRP, and coeliac antibodies in people meeting IBS criteria and identifies tests not required solely to confirm IBS.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management

investigations.text: Supports agreed symptom-response-based follow-up, annual review, and investigation or referral when new red-flag symptoms emerge.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped agreed follow-up, symptom-change review, safety, and clinician referral fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports agreed symptom-response-based follow-up, annual review, and investigation or referral when new red-flag symptoms emerge.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management

referral.text: Supports clinician-documented lifestyle, activity, diet, symptom-trigger, and specialist dietary discussion; specific advice is not inserted automatically.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-discussed lifestyle and dietary documentation without inserting specific advice automatically.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented lifestyle, activity, diet, symptom-trigger, and specialist dietary discussion; specific advice is not inserted automatically.

source.title: Irritable bowel syndrome in adults: diagnosis and management

source_limitations: People younger than 18 years and people not meeting symptom-duration and diagnostic criteria are outside the exact source.
Bloating and abdominal-cramping aliases are too broad for exact matching support.

Specific rectal bleeding, weight loss, nocturnal symptoms, family history, age-at-onset, anaemia, examination fields, stool studies, previous records, generic clinician plan, medication plan, fixed follow-up ranges, and PRN wording remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, red-flag interpretation, treatment, medicine dosing, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
